Currently, I bring 3+ years of experience to the table, transforming complex business requirements into seamless digital experiences.
bio.journey: I value consistency and continuous learning, dedicating myself to exploring new technologies, writing clean code, and sharing knowledge with the developer community through mentoring and technical training.

experience.title: Experience
experience.subtitle: A summary of my professional journey

bullets: Integrated Quran.com API via OAuth 2.0, reducing audio latency by 86% using custom in-memory caching.
Developed a reactive sync system with Stream Transformers for real-time verse metadata injection.
Engineered a Hybrid Audio Engine supporting dynamic fallback between local and cloud sources.
Authored 80+ unit & integration tests with Mocktail covering complex async repository logic.
Built a stress-monitoring app (HyperMate) connecting to an ESP8266 microcontroller via Wi-Fi with real-time GSR sensor readings and a smart chatbot.
Enhanced EcoSort — a recycling IoT client app — adding Firebase auth, push notifications, QR scanning, and Blync API integration for hardware sensors.
Delivered VolunTeen, a volunteering social platform for a real client with 30–40 responsive screens, AI chatbot (GPT-3), and WhatsApp integration.
Employed MVC Architecture with Provider state management across all freelance projects.
Developed 2 client Flutter applications (VolunTeen & Check-It), managing the complete SDLC from requirements to delivery.
Implemented barcode scanning, real-time price comparison, and e-commerce style product database in Check-It.
Integrated Firebase services, GPT-3 API, in-app video player, and third-party packages.
Delivered 50+ hours of hands-on Flutter training covering Dart, OOP, UI, layout, and navigation.
Worked in agile teams on 2 internal mobile apps and a hackathon E-wallet app, meeting tight sprint deadlines.
Translated Figma designs into pixel-perfect Flutter UI using MVVM architecture and Bloc Cubit state management.
Led community creation module for the Reddit Clone project — 6-member cross-functional team with Scrum methodology.
Delivered Python programming fundamentals to CS students, creating hands-on exercises covering data types, control flow, OOP, and problem-solving patterns.

ml.org: DEPI Initiative · Ministry of Communications & IT
bullets: Designed and delivered a comprehensive ML engineering curriculum — math foundations, Python ecosystem (NumPy/Pandas), and model training from scratch.
Led a 4-phase end-to-end capstone project lifecycle, culminating in a "sell your product" defense seminar.
Led a complete Flutter Development Diploma from zero to production, designing all course materials and handwritten notes.
Guided 35+ students from Dart basics through MVC architecture, state management, and real app deployment.

volunteen.title: VolunTeen
volunteen.subtitle: AI-Powered Volunteering platform for teenagers
volunteen.overview: A solo-developed Flutter application targeting teenagers with 30-40 screens, featuring AI chatbot integration and a complete volunteering management system.
volunteen.challenge: Designing a complex, feature-rich platform that remains engaging and simple enough for a teenage audience while integrating advanced AI capabilities.
volunteen.approach: Adopted an MVC architecture for maintainability. Implemented the full UI/UX provided by the client and integrated GPT-3 for a personalized assistant experience.
volunteen.solution: Successfully launched a professional-grade mobile app with a verified reward system for active volunteers and multiple awareness channels.
contributions: Developed 30-40 screens independently using Flutter/Dart
Implemented MVC architecture for scalable code structure
Integrated GPT-3 API for the intelligent volunteering assistant
Added awareness channels: Articles, Videos, and AI Assistant
Real people's support contacts and a community screen
Certification system, announcements, helping categories, and donation flow

checkit.title: Check-It
checkit.subtitle: Consumer Rights & Product Verification Prototype
checkit.overview: A specialized mobile prototype designed to empower consumers by providing instant product verification and a reporting mechanism for over-priced goods.
checkit.challenge: Creating a seamless bridge between physical barcodes and consumer protection databases while keeping the flow simple enough for the general public.
checkit.approach: Implemented a high-speed barcode scanning module and built a multi-step complaint wizard enabling users to file a complaint with product details, location, and receipt evidence.
checkit.solution: A production-ready utility prototype that facilitates consumer reporting and helps track illicit market pricing activity effectively.
contributions: Implemented high-performance barcode scanning using Flutter packages
Developed a state-driven multi-step reporting wizard with complaint tracking
Built local storage history and sync logic
Designed the UI/UX following clean material guidelines

ecosort.title: EcoSort — Feature Contribution
ecosort.subtitle: Feature contribution to a smart recycling app
ecosort.overview: Contributed new features to an existing client Flutter app: added Firebase authentication, push notifications, Blync API hardware integration for real-time sensor data, and a QR-based point reward system for recycling activities.
ecosort.challenge: Integrating a third-party IoT hardware API seamlessly into an existing codebase while building a reliable points system that ties sensor events to user actions.
ecosort.approach: Read and integrated the Blync REST API documentation to connect 3 hardware sensors in recycle bins. Used a QR scanning package to implement the recycling reward flow, and wired Firebase for auth and push notifications.
ecosort.solution: A set of well-integrated features that extended the existing app with real-time hardware connectivity and an engaging recycling reward mechanism.
contributions: Added Firebase authentication and push notifications to the existing app
Integrated Blync REST API to connect 3 real-time hardware sensors in recycle bins
Implemented QR code scanning to scan for reward points on recycling activities
Built the point calculation and reward tracking logic

hypermate.title: HyperMate IoT
hypermate.subtitle: Real-time stress monitoring via ESP8266
hypermate.overview: A solo client project bridging IoT hardware and mobile health tracking using GSR sensors connected to an ESP8266 microcontroller.
hypermate.challenge: Maintaining stable data streams from microcontrollers via Wi-Fi and providing actionable health insights in real-time.
hypermate.approach: Created a custom Wi-Fi service to communicate with the ESP8266 and implemented an AI-driven suggestion engine for user wellness recommendations.
hypermate.solution: A fully functional IoT health tracker that interprets physiological data to provide personalized stress-relief recommendations.
contributions: Built the custom Wi-Fi communication bridge for ESP8266
Developed real-time GSR sensor data visualization widgets
Integrated a wellness recommendation engine based on stress levels
Managed end-to-end solo development for a private client

tapcash.title: TapCash FinTech
tapcash.subtitle: Modern financial management and data visualization
tapcash.overview: A polished fintech app prototype demonstrating secure mobile payments, spending insights, and wallet management.
tapcash.challenge: Creating a premium UI with complex data visualizations that remains performant on varied mobile hardware.
tapcash.approach: Built a custom widget architecture for financial indicators and used specialized charting libraries for spending insights.
tapcash.solution: A high-fidelity prototype delivered in a 2-week hackathon, recognized for its exceptional UI/UX by industry judges.
contributions: Designed the primary financial dashboard and spending Insights section
Implemented custom bottom navigation and reusable card widgets
Built reactive UI components for balance tracking
Presented the solution at Orange Digital Center's Eid Hackathon

redditClone.title: Reddix — Reddit Clone
redditClone.subtitle: Full-featured social platform built in a team sprint
redditClone.overview: A complete Reddit mobile app clone developed as part of a software house team sprint following Agile methodology with MVC-S architecture. Owned the community management, settings, and authentication modules end-to-end.
redditClone.challenge: Delivering a production-grade social platform with community management, user settings, and auth flows in a team environment while following MVC-S architecture and avoiding state management conflicts across feature modules.
redditClone.approach: Owned three major modules independently: Community Creation (full CRUD flow, typed community forms, rules dialog), User Settings (account settings, email management, blocking/permissions), and Authentication (forgot password, username recovery, reset email confirmation).
redditClone.solution: A fully functional social ecosystem with live community creation, left/right drawer navigation, comprehensive account settings, and end-to-end auth recovery flows — demoed in a public YouTube Short.
contributions: Built the complete Community Creation flow: form validation, community type selection, rules dialog
Implemented left drawer with dynamic community list and right drawer with user actions
Developed full Settings & Account Settings modules (email, password, privacy, permissions)
Built Auth recovery flows: forgot password, username recovery, reset email confirmation
Configured Provider-based state management with MVC-S for zero cross-module conflicts

usAccidents.title: US Accidents Analysis
usAccidents.subtitle: Big Data & ML analysis of 7.7M traffic records
usAccidents.overview: A team project (2 members) analyzing the US Accidents dataset (7.7 million records, 49 columns) using PySpark on GCP. My role focused on the EDA and machine learning contribution — uncovering accident patterns, predicting severity, and building custom MapReduce algorithms.
usAccidents.challenge: Processing nearly 8 million records required distributed computing. The dataset had severe class imbalance and weak correlations for duration prediction, requiring careful feature analysis and model selection.
usAccidents.approach: Led the EDA phase with comprehensive data cleaning and exploration. Implemented custom MapReduce versions of Linear Regression and Logistic Regression from scratch. Trained and optimized ensemble models for severity classification.
usAccidents.solution: Logistic Regression and Random Forest achieved ~84% accuracy and F1-score ~0.77 for severity classification. Identified key insights: peak accident times (8-10 AM, 3-6 PM rush hours), high-risk areas (Miami highest accidents), and weather impacts (Light Blowing Snow highest average severity).
contributions: Led the EDA phase: comprehensive data cleaning, histograms, correlation matrices, geospatial visualizations
Implemented custom MapReduce Linear Regression and Logistic Regression from scratch using PySpark RDDs
Trained and hyperparameter-tuned Random Forest model achieving ~84% accuracy, F1 ~0.77
Applied K-Means clustering of accident locations for geographic risk analysis
Identified key risk patterns: rush-hour peaks, weather severity correlations, high-risk cities

vpredicto.title: Predicto
vpredicto.subtitle: Graduation Project — Open-source Video Frame Prediction library
vpredicto.overview: As team leader of Graduation Project Team 18, led the development of Predicto — an open-source Python library (PyTorch + Tkinter) that makes Video Frame Prediction (VFP) accessible to both non-experts and ML specialists. The library provides a desktop GUI for drag-and-drop video input, model selection, prediction, and visualization, alongside a clean API for expert use.
vpredicto.challenge: Unifying disparate research architectures (SimVP, PredNet, ConvLSTM, PredRNN++) into a single cohesive API while building a cross-platform desktop GUI and resolving weeks of dimension-mismatch bugs in ConvLSTM-based models.
vpredicto.approach: Owned full design and implementation of the Desktop App (GUI): splash screen, main screen with side menu, video frame display, playback controls, file handling, model processing, and visualization modules. Implemented SimVP (CNN Encoder-Translator-Decoder with TPU optimization and checkpointing for 5× speedup) and PredNet (Predictive Coding Network with ConvLSTM, resolving weeks of dimension-mismatch debugging).
vpredicto.solution: A functional, benchmarked open-source library supporting multiple models with state-of-the-art SSIM scores. Awarded A+ grade. Documented in the team book and published as an extensible tool bridging research and practical applications in autonomous driving, robotics, and video.
contributions: Led team of 4: organized learning roadmap (Coursera ML/DL, PyTorch), Trello boards, deadlines, meeting docs
Designed and fully implemented the Desktop App GUI — 7 modules including splash, side menu, playback, visualization
Implemented SimVP model: CNN Encoder-Translator-Decoder with TPU optimization and checkpointing (5× speedup)
Implemented PredNet: Predictive Coding Network with ConvLSTM, resolved weeks of dimension-mismatch debugging
Maintained a daily devlog, authored multiple book chapters, and integrated all models into the desktop app

phishing.title: Phishing Detection
phishing.subtitle: ML-based web phishing classifier — team project
phishing.overview: A team machine learning project tackling phishing detection using a balanced Kaggle dataset of web page features. Conducted thorough EDA, preprocessing, and trained multiple classifiers to achieve state-of-the-art accuracy.
phishing.challenge: Phishing attacks are highly sophisticated — 97% of security experts fail to identify them. The dataset required careful feature selection with web_traffic showing significant outliers and many low-correlation features needing removal.
phishing.approach: Conducted EDA (histograms, correlation matrices, box plots). Preprocessed by scaling, fixing negatives, and dropping features with correlation < 0.1. Trained and cross-validated multiple models including Random Forest, SVM, AdaBoost, and compared against baselines.
phishing.solution: Top-performing models: Random Forest (96.8%), SVM with tuning (96.7%), AdaBoost (96.8%). F1-scores ~0.97, precision/recall ~0.96-0.97, significantly outperforming baselines like ZeroR (49%) and Logistic Regression (95%).
contributions: Conducted EDA: histograms, correlation matrices, box plots (key: google_index at 0.73 correlation)
Preprocessed data: scaling, fixing negatives, dropping low-correlation features
Trained and evaluated Random Forest, SVM, AdaBoost, Logistic Regression via cross-validation
Applied hyperparameter tuning (RandomizedSearchCV) for optimal model performance
Comparative evaluation of 5+ classifiers and documented findings

lineFollower.title: Maze-Solver Robot App
lineFollower.subtitle: Native Android app for autonomous robot navigation
lineFollower.overview: A native Android app powering a Maze-Solver Robot with real-time computer vision via OpenCV for line following and maze navigation modes, communicating wirelessly with an ESP32 microcontroller to control robot speed and actions.
lineFollower.challenge: Processing camera frames in real-time on mobile hardware using native C++ while maintaining non-blocking UI and reliable wireless communication with the robot.
lineFollower.approach: Built C++ native modules via JNI for high-performance OpenCV processing (car detection, corner/line detection, adaptive thresholding, distance calculations). Used threading for non-blocking UI and HTTP POST requests to ESP32 endpoints for motor control.
lineFollower.solution: A real-time robot control app supporting both maze navigation (corner detection, debounce signals) and line following (adaptive lines, first-detection handling) with PID-based motor control.
contributions: Implemented C++ native modules via JNI: car detection, corner/line detection, adaptive thresholding, distance calculations
Built UI with PID constant controls (kd, kv, ki), mode toggle, start/capture/demo buttons
Implemented ESP32 connectivity and HTTP POST commands to /maxminspeed, /linefollowerminspeed endpoints
Added threading for non-blocking UI, camera permissions, and screen-wake logic

mipsProcessor.title: MIPS 5-Stage Processor
mipsProcessor.subtitle: Lead Processor Architect — Decode & Integration
mipsProcessor.overview: A hardware-level team project implementing a full pipelined MIPS CPU in Verilog with 5 pipeline stages, advanced hazard resolution via forwarding, and top-level integration. My role was Lead Processor Architect for the Decode stage and system integration.
mipsProcessor.challenge: Synchronizing 5 pipeline stages through robust intermediate buffers while resolving data and control hazards without excessive stalling across all instruction types.
mipsProcessor.approach: Designed the top-level integration (MIPS_phase_3.v) syncing all 5 stages, implemented the instruction decoder (Decode2.v) mapping the ISA to internal control signals, and integrated the Forwarding and Hazard Detection units.
mipsProcessor.solution: A functional pipelined CPU verified against sorting and mathematical programs on ModelSim, achieving correct execution across all implemented MIPS instructions.
contributions: Designed top-level integration (MIPS_phase_3.v) synchronizing all 5 stages via intermediate buffers
Implemented the Decode stage (Decode2.v): instruction lookup mapping ISA to ALU, MEM, and STACK control signals
Generated all 15 core control signals for the execution pipeline
Integrated and verified Forwarding and Hazard Detection units for single-cycle dependency handling
Verified correctness using ModelSim waveform analysis across multiple test programs

logicSimulator.title: Logic Gate Simulator
logicSimulator.subtitle: Interactive GUI for digital circuit design — team of 4
logicSimulator.overview: A Windows desktop application in C++ using CMU Graphics library for designing and simulating digital logic circuits, developed as a 4-person team. Features include drag-and-drop gate placement, real-time simulation, truth table generation, save/load, undo/redo, copy/cut/paste, and switch toggling.
logicSimulator.challenge: Building a robust event-driven architecture separating input handling, application logic, and rendering while supporting complex interactions like multi-level undo/redo that don't corrupt circuit state.
logicSimulator.approach: Implemented the core getUserAction() for precise input handling across design/simulation modes, and ExecuteAction() in ApplicationManager as a central command dispatcher using the Strategy Pattern. Used file I/O for circuit persistence.
logicSimulator.solution: A polished educational tool supporting multi-input gates (AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR, buffers), truth table generation, real-time simulation, and full binary file persistence.
contributions: Implemented getUserAction() for precise input handling across design and simulation modes
Built ExecuteAction() in ApplicationManager as central command dispatcher using Strategy Pattern
Developed Save/Load classes with binary file I/O for full circuit persistence (components + connections)
Enhanced ChangeSwitch class with toggle logic and bounds checking
Contributed UI improvements including gate toolbar toggling and Exit class

osScheduler.title: OS Process Scheduler
osScheduler.subtitle: OS simulation in C with Buddy Allocator memory management
osScheduler.overview: A complete Operating System simulation in C modeling kernel-level process management, synchronization, and resource allocation using Linux system calls. Personally led the Process Generator and the Dynamic Memory Allocation system.
osScheduler.challenge: Simulating high-concurrency process flows with real-time IPC communication while implementing a sound memory allocator that minimizes external fragmentation across multiple concurrent processes.
osScheduler.approach: Built the Process Generator as the system's central controller: parsing process data, managing clock and scheduler via fork() and exec(), and coordinating process arrival via IPC Message Queues. Designed a Buddy Allocator managing a 1024-byte memory pool through recursive block splitting and buddy merging.
osScheduler.solution: A robust OS simulator handling HPF, SRTN, and Round Robin scheduling algorithms with precise memory allocation, IPC coordination, and performance metrics output.
contributions: Implemented the Process Generator: process data parsing, fork()/exec() lifecycle management, IPC coordination
Designed and built the Buddy Allocator: allocate() and deallocate() with recursive 1024-byte pool management
Implemented buddy merging to minimize external fragmentation across concurrent process scenarios
Led the integration phase ensuring seamless communication between clock, scheduler, and generator modules
System supports HPF, SRTN, and Round Robin scheduling with turnaround/wait time performance metrics

searchEngine.title: Search Engine — Web Crawler
searchEngine.subtitle: Multithreaded Java web crawler module
searchEngine.overview: Contributed the core Web Crawler backend module of a full Java search engine. The crawler serves as the entry point for the entire pipeline, feeding indexed pages to the ranking and retrieval layers.
searchEngine.challenge: Managing thousands of concurrent network requests while ensuring ethical crawling (robots.txt compliance), URL deduplication, and fault-tolerant resumption across interruptions.
searchEngine.approach: Designed a multithreaded crawler using Java's ExecutorService for parallel fetching. Implemented a disk-persistent BFS queue for fault tolerance, URL canonicalization for deduplication, and robots.txt parsing. Stored crawled data in MongoDB with page title, URL, compact string, and popularity metrics.
searchEngine.solution: A fault-tolerant crawler supporting session resumption, unique content verification, and scalable BFS traversal up to the configured max count.
contributions: Designed and implemented the multithreaded web crawler using Java ExecutorService
Built disk-persistent BFS queue for fault-tolerant resumption of interrupted crawl sessions
Developed URL canonicalization to prevent duplicate processing and infinite redirect loops
Integrated robots.txt parser for ethical crawling compliance
Stored crawled pages in MongoDB with title, URL, compact string, and popularity tracking

goDfs.title: Distributed File System
goDfs.subtitle: Fault-tolerant DFS with hybrid gRPC + TCP protocol
goDfs.overview: A high-performance, fault-tolerant distributed file system (Go-DFS) designed for large-scale video storage using a Master-Worker (Tracker-Keeper) architecture. Deliberately chose a hybrid protocol: gRPC for control-plane reliability and raw TCP for high-speed data transfers — demonstrating deliberate architectural thinking.
goDfs.challenge: Building a resilient distributed system that maintains data integrity during node failures, enforces replication across nodes, and supports high-throughput MP4 streaming without sacrificing management reliability.
goDfs.approach: Implemented the gRPC + Protocol Buffers control plane for heartbeat monitoring with timeout mechanisms, and raw TCP sockets for the high-speed data plane. Built the Master's auto-replication engine, Data Keeper self-scanning/registration, and the upload/download pipeline with multi-stage Master-Client handshake.
goDfs.solution: A concurrent, resilient distributed file system with auto failure detection, seamless data recovery, enforced replication factor of 3, and real-time MP4 video streaming.
contributions: Implemented gRPC + Protocol Buffers control plane for heartbeat monitoring with timeout-based failure detection
Built the Master's auto-replication engine enforcing a replication factor of 3 across the cluster
Developed Data Keeper self-scanning and registration for dynamic node discovery
Implemented upload/download pipelines with multi-stage Master-Client direct P2P transfer handshake
Deliberate hybrid protocol design: gRPC for reliability, TCP for maximum data-plane throughput

rsaChat.title: RSA Encrypted Chat
rsaChat.subtitle: End-to-end encrypted messaging from scratch
rsaChat.overview: A secure messaging application implementing RSA encryption from mathematical primitives using Python sockets for truly private communication.
rsaChat.challenge: Implementing RSA key generation, encryption, and decryption from scratch without relying on cryptography libraries — using only prime generation and modular exponentiation.
rsaChat.approach: Built the entire RSA pipeline: prime generation, modular exponentiation, key pair creation, and socket-based message exchange.
rsaChat.solution: A functional E2E encrypted chat system demonstrating deep understanding of public-key cryptography fundamentals.
contributions: Implemented RSA key generation with prime number testing from primitives
Built modular exponentiation for efficient encryption and decryption
Designed the TCP socket-based message exchange protocol
Created the full encrypt-transmit-decrypt pipeline

gamingo.title: Gamingo Game Engine
gamingo.subtitle: Custom 3D game engine in C++ & OpenGL
gamingo.overview: Core engine developer for Gamingo — a custom 3D game engine built from scratch using C++ and OpenGL, and its flagship title 'Drive Me Home'. Designed fundamental graphics pipeline components and implemented the game's visual and state logic.
gamingo.challenge: Building a performant OpenGL rendering pipeline, collision detection system, and complex game state machine that work cohesively for real-time 3D gameplay across Past, Present, and Future narrative levels.
gamingo.approach: Designed Shader, Texture, and Material classes with GLSL for custom programmable shading. Implemented TexStorage2D for efficient texture memory. Engineered AABB/Sphere collision detection and the state machine for Menu, Level-Up, and Game-Over transitions. Authored post-processing shaders (Sepia, Inversion, CRT, night-time filter).
gamingo.solution: A playable 3D game engine capable of rendering asset-heavy scenes at high frame rates, with cohesive team integration, visual post-processing per level, and robust game state management.
contributions: Designed and implemented Shader, Texture, and Material pipeline classes with custom GLSL shading
Built TexStorage2D utility for efficient GPU texture memory allocation
Implemented AABB/Sphere collision detection system using GLM linear algebra
Engineered the Game State Manager handling non-linear Menu, Level-Up, and Game-Over transitions
Authored Sepia, Inversion, and CRT post-processing shaders plus dynamic night-time filter
Spearheaded final integration: level navigation, Finish component, and team asset merge-coordination

marsExploration.title: Mars Exploration Simulation
marsExploration.subtitle: Event-driven mission scheduling with custom data structures
marsExploration.overview: An event-driven simulation system in C++ optimizing mission scheduling and resource management for a Mars exploration station. Models complex interactions between Emergency, Mountainous, and Polar mission types and a fleet of rovers with distinct states.
marsExploration.challenge: Efficiently scheduling concurrent rover missions with varying priorities while managing rover state transitions, maintenance cycles, and real-time event logging — without relying on STL containers.
marsExploration.approach: Built generic LinkedQueue and PriorityQueue data structures from scratch for maximum memory control. Designed a multi-variable priority equation dynamically balancing mission urgency and formulation time. Authored the Rover class hierarchy with state-transition and post-mission deallocation logic.
marsExploration.solution: A robust simulation engine handling multiple concurrent Mars missions with optimal resource allocation, real-time event dashboard, automated reporting, and verified edge-case handling.
contributions: Implemented generic LinkedQueue and PriorityQueue from scratch — no STL, full memory control
Designed the core mission priority equation: weighted multi-variable formula balancing urgency and formulation time
Authored complete Rover class hierarchy: availability, execution, and maintenance state transitions
Built the automated reporting system outputting mission statistics and completion metrics
Developed the interactive UI dashboard for real-time event logging and simulation monitoring
Spearheaded verification phase with rigorous test cases for mission cancellations and concurrent rover failures

mushafImad.title: Mushaf-IMAD Flutter
mushafImad.subtitle: Open-source Quran audio integration — Itqan Community
mushafImad.overview: Contributed to an open-source Quran reader Flutter package by integrating the Quran.com API as a full audio source. The contribution spans OAuth 2.0 authentication, a reactive streaming sync system, a hybrid audio engine, and a comprehensive test suite of 80+ tests using Mocktail.
mushafImad.challenge: The existing package had no cloud audio integration. Designing a seamless hybrid system that dynamically falls back between local and cloud sources while maintaining zero-latency verse-to-audio sync.
mushafImad.approach: Implemented OAuth 2.0 token management, built a reactive verse metadata injection pipeline using Stream Transformers, and created an in-memory caching layer that reduced API latency by 86%. All logic was driven by TDD with Mocktail.
mushafImad.solution: A robust hybrid audio engine with a RAM → Local → API timing hierarchy ensuring zero-latency playback sync, and architectural walkthroughs published for long-term community maintainability.
contributions: Integrated Quran.com API via OAuth 2.0 with custom in-memory caching (86% latency reduction)
Developed reactive sync system with Stream Transformers for real-time verse metadata injection
Engineered a Hybrid Audio Engine with dynamic fallback between local and cloud sources
Authored 80+ unit and integration tests using Mocktail for complex async repository logic
Published architectural guides and walkthroughs for community maintainability
